failed_entry.error: gpu-first audit object vector differs from captured CPU oracle
failed_entry.reason: gpu-first audit object vector differs from captured CPU oracle

selected supported shape evaluated by worker

selected_entries.error: gpu-first audit object vector differs from captured CPU oracle
selected_entries.reason: gpu-first audit object vector differs from captured CPU oracle

substitution_json.error: gpu-first audit object vector differs from captured CPU oracle

benefit_assessment_plain_language: M4x proves the first real GPU-first graph-facing shortcut: after the verified session batch exists, query 353 returned the cached worker OverlapRange vector with cpu_overlap_ms=0 and worker_process_ms=0, and the full toy-raw artifacts still matched CPU. The audit negative proves a forced GPU-first mismatch fails closed before graph mutation. This is a seam-level benefit, not an end-to-end speedup yet: CPU toy-raw took 72s and M4x positive took 84s because only one selected cached overlap was bypassed while the rest of Flye remains CPU-bound.

claim_boundary.allowed: cuFlye can reuse a verified file-backed CUDA session batch cache for a later allowlisted supported overlap query before live CPU overlap, preserve exact toy-raw artifacts, and fail closed on GPU-first audit mismatch.
claim_boundary.forbidden: M4x does not prove default GPU mode, broad unsupported-shape substitution, or end-to-end Flye speedup.
